ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic burrito entry; no specific Paneer Tikka Burrito entry exists. Mapped to closest parent class 'burrito (food product)' as an approximate match.
ontology.foodex2_description: Filled wheat flour tortilla (burrito-type composite dish)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated Paneer Tikka Burrito code. A0FKP (filled wheat flour tortilla) is the closest defensible composite-dish anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for this specific fusion dish; omitted to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Paneer Tikka Burrito weighs approximately 200 g, providing around 498 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Paneer Tikka Burritos — Singapore Health Promotion Board (HPB) Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Foodo database record for Paneer Tikka Burritos (CR ID: G00010825) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from ICMR-NIN paneer composition data and USDA FoodData Central dairy component data as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown separately for this dish; see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; wheat flour tortilla and mixed Indian curry dish analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Paneer Tikka Burrito. GI estimated at 62 (medium) based on analogous composite dish reasoning: wheat flour tortilla GI ~52–58 (University of Sydney GI Database), combined with paneer tikka curry component GI ~55–65 (mixed Indian spiced dish analogue). Weighted average of carbohydrate-contributing components yields an estimated GI of approximately 62. Glycemic load per 200 g serving = (62 × 20.06 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 2) / 100 = 24.9 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 470 mg/100 g with low potassium offset (126 mg/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to no more than 100 g and request low-sodium tikka seasoning, as the sodium-to-potassium ratio of approximately 3.7:1 significantly exceeds the cardioprotective target of below 1:1, increasing vasopressor load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined carbohydrate 20.06 g/100 g with low dietary fibre 1.4 g/100 g; estimated GI 62 (medium); glycemic load 24.9 per standard serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid regular consumption of this dish due to its high glycemic load of approximately 24.9 per serving; substituting the white flour tortilla with a high-fibre whole-wheat or low-GI wrap and increasing vegetable content to raise fibre above 3 g/100 g would meaningfully reduce postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~6.5 g/100 g and cholesterol 212 mg/100 g from paneer, cheese, and sour cream
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this dish due to its high saturated fat content (~6.5 g/100 g) and elevated cholesterol (212 mg/100 g), both of which contribute to LDL elevation and atherogenic risk; reducing cheese and sour cream and replacing with low-fat yogurt would lower saturated fat below the 5 g/100 g threshold.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~6.5 g/100 g exceeds 5 g/100 g RA threshold; nightshade vegetables (bell peppers, tomatoes) may trigger flares in sensitive individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should reduce cheese and sour cream to lower saturated fat below 5 g/100 g, and monitor for individual sensitivity to nightshade vegetables present in the salsa component, while noting that turmeric in tikka spices provides modest anti-inflammatory benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Paneer Tikka Burrito is an Indian-Mexican fusion dish popular in Singapore's multicultural food scene. It consists of a large wheat flour tortilla filled with grilled paneer (fresh Indian cottage cheese) marinated in tikka spice paste — a blend of yogurt, cumin, coriander, turmeric, chilli, and garam masala — along with sautéed bell peppers, onion, tomato salsa, shredded cheese, and sour cream. The paneer is typically grilled or charred before assembly, imparting a smoky, spiced flavour with a soft, slightly chewy texture. The burrito is then rolled and may be lightly toasted. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 249 kcal, 10.91 g protein, 13.71 g fat (estimated saturated fat ~6.5 g), 20.06 g carbohydrate, 1.4 g dietary fibre, 470 mg sodium, and 212 mg cholesterol. The high saturated fat and sodium content make it a concern for cardiovascular disease, hypertension, and rheumatoid arthritis. The refined carbohydrate-to-fibre ratio and estimated glycemic index of 62 (medium) with a glycemic load of approximately 24.9 per 200 g serving make it unsuitable for regular consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. It is vegetarian but not vegan, halal status depends on preparation. Common in Singapore hawker fusion stalls and café menus.

Is paneer tikka burrito suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from HPB Foodo Singapore database (CR ID G00010825, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat estimated from ICMR-NIN and USDA FoodData Central dairy components as HPB does not report saturated fat separately for this dish. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database composite dish analogues. All other HPB-reported micronutrients preserved at source confidence.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (6.5 g/100 g) is an estimate derived from ICMR-NIN paneer composition (approximately 4.5 g saturated fat per 100 g paneer) and USDA FoodData Central data for cheddar cheese and sour cream, weighted by approximate dish proportions. HPB does not publish saturated fat breakdown for this specific dish. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific fusion dish preparation are not available in the HPB Foodo database, ICMR-NIN, or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 3. GI of 62 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Paneer Tikka Burrito exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. Estimation basis: wheat flour tortilla GI ~52–58 combined with mixed Indian spiced paneer dish analogue GI ~55–65. 4. Halal status is preparation-dependent; paneer and cheese sourcing must be verified for halal certification. 5. The input record used the spelling 'Panner Tikka Burritos'; the canonical English name is corrected to 'Paneer Tikka Burrito' (standard spelling). 6. Cholesterol at 212 mg/100 g is notably high, consistent with paneer and dairy-rich composition.
